Our journeys

Four ways to
experience Italy.
All unhurried.

Each journey is personally hosted, intimately sized, and built around the places no map will ever show you. Find the one that calls to you — or let us help you choose.

When

April – October

Franciacorta.
Brescia. Lake Iseo.

Italy's sparkling heart, where vineyards meet the water. Ride through Franciacorta on e-bikes, taste at four private cellars, wander a car-free island on Lake Iseo, and sit down to long lunches that no guidebook will ever list.

  • Private tastings at Bersi Serlini, Berlucchi, Santus & Monte Rossa
  • E-bike day through Franciacorta vineyards
  • Afternoon on Monte Isola — Italy's largest lake island
  • Biodynamic farm visit at Il Colmetto
Duration 5 days / 4 nights
Group size Max 8 guests
Season Apr – Oct
Discover →

April – October

Lake Garda.
Verona. Valpolicella.

Verona's ancient streets, the rolling hills of Valpolicella, the calm of Lake Garda. Amarone tasted where it's born, a Roman amphitheatre at dusk, and the kind of beauty that stops you mid-sentence.

  • Private Amarone tasting in Valpolicella
  • Guided walk through Verona's historic centre
  • Boat trip on Lake Garda to hidden villages
  • Wellness morning overlooking the lake
Duration 5 days / 4 nights
Group size Max 8 guests
Season Apr – Oct
Discover →

April – October

Parma.
Mantova. Cremona.

Medieval grandeur meets Italy's greatest food culture. Prosciutto and Parmigiano tasted where they're made, with the people who make them. Lambrusco on the hills of Mantova. UNESCO heritage at every turn.

  • Behind-the-scenes at a Prosciutto di Parma producer
  • Parmigiano-Reggiano dairy visit and tasting
  • Guided tour of Mantova's Gonzaga palaces
  • Lambrusco tasting in the Mantovan hills
Duration 5 days / 4 nights
Group size Max 8 guests
Season Apr – Oct
Discover →
